title: Planet Grows Revenue 15% in FY 24, Makes Changes to Executive Team
text: Planet Labs increased fiscal 2024 revenue to $220.7 million, reflecting 15% year-over-year growth. The satellite imagery company reported a non-GAAP gross margin of 54% for the full year, with adjusted EBITDA losses narrowing to $55.3 million. CEO Will Marshall noted that Growth for the year was led by the Government sector, with defense and intelligence revenue surging over 30% annually. The organization surpassed a significant customer milestone by reaching 1,018 clients, a 15% increase from the prior year. Kevin Weil, President of Product and Business, is departing his operational role but will remain as an advisor. CFO Ashley Johnson will assume the president title alongside her financial leadership role. Additionally, Robert Cardillo, former director of the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency, will oversee the company's global defense and intelligence strategy. Planet expects first-quarter fiscal 2025 revenue between $58-61 million, representing 10-16% year-over-year growth.
